_comment: Default MCP servers bundled with Ember. User ~/.ember/mcp.json and project .ember/mcp.json take precedence.

scaffold.description: Curated project scaffolding and post-setup handoff generation.
scaffold._comment: Deterministic project scaffolding for coordinator, director, and ops. Coordinators can scaffold first and then hand off when deeper implementation is needed.

playwright.description: Browser automation for login flows, interaction, screenshots, and verification.
playwright._comment: Override headless/headed per environment via project .ember/mcp.json. Add --no-sandbox in Docker/CI. Add --caps vision for screenshot-heavy flows.

desktop.description: Cross-platform desktop automation for screenshots, app launching, focus, keyboard, and mouse control.
desktop._comment: Desktop capabilities vary by OS and local permissions. Call describe_environment first to learn the active surface.

filesystem.description: Workspace filesystem access for direct file inspection and edits alongside Ember's native file tools.
filesystem._comment: Uses the workspace root as the allowed filesystem root. Terminal access remains available via Ember's native terminal tool.

memory.description: Persistent knowledge graph for structured entity/relation storage. Complements Ember's flat memory with graph queries.
memory._comment: Stores entities and relations in a local knowledge graph. Good for tracking complex project relationships, architecture maps, and cross-session context that benefits from graph structure.

github.description: GitHub API access — repos, issues, PRs, branches, file contents, and code search.
github._comment: Set GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN in env or ~/.ember/mcp.json to enable. Without a token the server will start but most operations will fail.

sequential-thinking.description: Structured multi-step reasoning tool for complex problem decomposition, planning, and analysis.
sequential-thinking._comment: Provides a sequential thinking tool that helps break down complex problems step by step. Useful for architecture decisions, debugging strategies, and multi-part analysis.

brave-search.description: Web and local search via the Brave Search API.
brave-search._comment: Requires BRAVE_API_KEY. Disabled by default — enable in ~/.ember/mcp.json or .ember/mcp.json with your key. Complements Ember's native web_search with Brave's index.

postgres.description: Read-only PostgreSQL access — schema inspection and SELECT queries.
postgres._comment: Requires a postgres:// connection string as the last arg. Disabled by default — enable in ~/.ember/mcp.json with your connection string. Read-only by default for safety.

gitlab.description: GitLab API access — projects, issues, merge requests, and repository operations.
gitlab._comment: Requires GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN. Disabled by default — enable in ~/.ember/mcp.json with your token. Set GITLAB_API_URL for self-hosted instances.

slack.description: Slack workspace access — read channels, post messages, search conversations, and manage threads.
slack._comment: Requires SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_TEAM_ID. Disabled by default — enable in ~/.ember/mcp.json with your workspace credentials.

google-maps.description: Google Maps API — geocoding, directions, place search, and distance calculations.
google-maps._comment: Requires GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY. Disabled by default — enable when location/mapping features are needed.

gmail.description: Gmail email management — read inbox, search, send, reply, manage labels and categories via IMAP/SMTP.
gmail._comment: Requires Gmail address and App Password (not regular password). Create an App Password at https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords. Enable 2FA first.

outlook-mail.description: Outlook/Microsoft 365 email — read, send, search, and manage emails via Microsoft Graph API with Device Flow auth.
outlook-mail._comment: Requires Azure AD app registration. Create one at https://portal.azure.com. Set MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID and MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID.
